D11 ComputationalL3 ApplyReference

Box Filter — Simple Averaging

k × k box kernel replaces each pixel with the local mean; O(1) per pixel using integral images, but creates blocky artifacts vs Gaussian's smooth fall-off

Box filter has a flat spectral response — passes some high frequencies that Gaussian suppresses. Result: visible "ringing" at sharp edges and a noticeably square-shaped blur. Used in real-time graphics for cheap blur, and as a building block for separable filters via repeated application.

Slide kernel size k.
